title: N=8 swarm perf validation + HBR-SWARM-001/002/003/004 evidence run
goal: Per spec §6.7.3 (AC-SWARM-HARNESS-N8) validate perf scaling to N=8 minimum. Configure the swarm harness with N=8 concurrent governed sessions running KernelActionCatalogV1 mutations against a shared workspace; assert no silent last-writer-wins, no lease starvation, no deadlock, all conflicts surface as deterministic conflict reports or revision rejections.
expected_diff_shape: New test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/swarm_n8_perf_tests.rs with N=8 scenario; perf metric collection; gov-check sub-check that runs the N=8 perf as the canonical HBR-SWARM-001..004 evidence.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.7.3. N=8 scenario: each session runs 100 KernelActionCatalogV1 mutations against a shared CRDT workspace (KERNEL-002 primitive). Mix of mutation kinds: 60% additions (CRDT merge-safe), 20% updates on contested fields (must surface as conflict reports per HBR-SWARM-001), 10% lease-acquisition attempts on a single shared resource (must show lease starvation absence — every session eventually gets the lease per HBR-SWARM-002), 10% cancellation mid-mutation (must show bounded cancellation propagation per HBR-SWARM-002). Assertions: (a) ZERO silent overwrites (verified by hashing pre/post state and confirming every conflict produced a deterministic ConflictReport row); (b) max lease wait time across sessions < 5s (no starvation); (c) no deadlock (all sessions complete in bounded time); (d) all conflicts surface in EventLedger as `CRDT_CONFLICT_REPORT` rows or `REVISION_REJECTION` rows. Perf metrics: per-session p50/p95/p99 step duration; total run wall-clock; ledger overflow count (should be 0 with default capacity 1024 for N=8 * 100 steps). The Node helper `hbr-swarm-n8.mjs` is the gov-check sub-check that spawns the cargo test and parses the result. Justfile recipe `hbr-swarm-n8` wired into gov-check umbrella. Per HBR-SWARM-001/002/003/004 evidence_kind this is THE wire test producing the swarm evidence.
risk_if_missed: Without N=8 evidence, HBR-SWARM-001/002/003/004 are unverified at the acceptance perf floor; concurrency regressions ship invisibly.
validator_focus: Conflict rows are deterministic (re-run produces same conflict signature); lease starvation test asserts max wait time; cancellation test asserts bounded propagation; ledger overflow count is 0 at this load.

minimum_controls: Conflicts are deterministic (re-run with same seed produces same conflict signature).
Lease starvation test asserts a MAX wait time, not a sum or average.
Ledger overflow count asserted == 0 at N=8 * 100 load.
